title: Merging law and economics
primary_topics: liberty and prosperity under the rule of law
sub_topics: Global Forum on Liberty and Prosperity
Bill of Rights vs. social/economic rights as constitutional categories

stances.claim: Humans need both political and economic freedoms together — justice and jobs, freedom and food, ethics and economics, liberty and prosperity — one is useless without the other.
stances.rhetorical_move: rhetorical pairing / chiasmic enumeration ('twin beacons') as core philosophical formula

stances.claim: The best way to conquer poverty, create wealth, and share prosperity is to unleash the entrepreneurial genius of people by granting them freedom and tools to help themselves and society.
stances.rhetorical_move: policy desideratum stated as universal truth

stances.claim: Tañada v. Angara was the proper jurisprudential turn away from the isolationist 'Filipino First' doctrine toward globalization, liberalization, deregulation, and privatization.
stances.rhetorical_move: self-citation of his own ponencia + personal anecdote of persuading colleagues from 3 to 15

stances.claim: After his retirement, the Supreme Court reverted to prioritizing political liberties over economic prosperity — a regression attributable to the Constitution's structural asymmetry, since Bill of Rights provisions are mandatory and self-executing while social and economic rights are listed as mere aspirations needing implementing laws.
stances.rhetorical_move: constitutional structural analysis + historical explanation (Constitution born from anti-colonial struggle)

institutions: Global Forum on Liberty and Prosperity
events: Global Forum on Liberty and Prosperity (2006)

signature_phrases: twin beacons must always go together; one is useless without the other
unleash the entrepreneurial genius of people
liberty and prosperity under the rule of law
justice and jobs; freedom and food; ethics and economics; peace and development
the egalitarian goal should be achieved only via libertarian methods
register_markers: I had difficulty persuading my colleagues
decision_framework_signals: applies 'liberty and prosperity together' lens to constitutional interpretation — argues Bill of Rights primacy must be balanced by judicially enforceable economic rights
frames WTO/globalization as legally correct outcome under his framework, against contemporary 'Filipino First' resistance
structural-constitutional reading: distinguishes self-executing from aspirational provisions to explain why economic rights get shelved
cross_references: In my own books, speeches and decisions, I espoused a common theme
Tañada v. Angara (May 2, 1997) — his own ponencia
notable_anecdotes: persuading 14 colleagues from initial 3-vote support to unanimous concurrence in Tañada v. Angara
Canadian CJ Beverley McLachlin proposing to rename the philosophy 'liberty and prosperity under the rule of law' at the Global Forum
